target.name: Sila Nanotechnologies
target.founder: Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, Gene Berdichevsky

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sila Nanotechnologies is a late-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, and Gene Berdichevsky. The target has raised $375M and carries a current post-money valuation of $3.3B. The peer universe consists of 9 comparable companies—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private entities in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages, including TAE Technologies, Form Energy, Ascend Elements, and Watershed.

Two valuation methods triangulate the range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Late Stage advancement to the $3.3B base, yielding $4.0B. Peer-median methodology anchors to four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $3.2B and a spread from $1.8B to $6.0B. These two approaches yield implied valuations of $4.0B and $3.2B respectively.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $4.6B, with a median of $3.6B. This range reflects the current $3.3B valuation near the lower bound. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion (6.0x spread between TAE Technologies at $6.0B and Watershed at $1.8B) and limited public market comparables for validation. Stage-progression assumes standard advancement probability; peer-median sensitivity to company-specific technology maturity and go-to-market trajectory remains material.
